codeReview.summary: The feature adds a dedicated parameterized matrix and reviewer-scope tests, but it does not satisfy the mission contract requirement to assert recovery kind semantics, and one of the intended nine rules is not uniquely protected against silent regression.

issues.description: The matrix assertions validate `result.recovery?.errorCode` rather than `result.recovery.kind`. This does not meet the stated requirement for VAL-CORRECT-023..031 to assert exact recovery kind (`validation-contract.md:213-255`, `features.json:1157`). Because kind-level behavior is not asserted, refactors that preserve errorCode mapping but alter completion-check kind semantics can slip through.

The case labeled `required final review not passing` is not isolated from the earlier optional-final-review guard. In `validateSuccessfulCompletion`, the optional check at `execution-completion-guards.ts:95` runs before the `requireFinalReview` check at line 113, and both map to `failing_final_review`. As written, this test still passes if the dedicated `requireFinalReview` check is removed, so the 9-rule matrix does not fully guarantee that this specific check cannot be silently dropped.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission contracts/specs for completion gates still require asserting `recovery.kind`, but transition recovery currently exposes `errorCode` (no `kind` field).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md:213-255` and `features.json:1157` require kind assertions; `src/runtime/transitions/shared.ts:13-14` defines `TransitionRecovery` with `errorCode` and no `kind`. The worker handoff explicitly notes they changed expectations from kind to errorCode after red tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Core-worker procedure compliance appears overstated for this run: required orientation doc `user-testing.md` was not read in the transcript skeleton, but handoff marks followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `core-worker/SKILL.md:24` requires reading `.factory/library/user-testing.md`; transcript skeleton for session `5c37f985-db3e-4ce5-9de6-575c15e6f591` shows `architecture.md` read and `followedProcedure: true`, with no `user-testing.md` read entry.

summary: Fail: although useful new tests were added, this feature misses the contract-level kind assertion requirement and does not uniquely protect the required-final-review gate from silent regression.
